# PAO v0.3.0 Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** Implement 5 backlog items: compaction trace model, eviction eligibility, cross-session resume, HasKey identity contracts, and lifecycle transition semantics.

**Architecture:** Programmatic build via `scripts/build.py` (rdflib). All classes, properties, axioms, ABox data, and SHACL shapes are generated by Python code. Tests in `tests/test_ontology.py` validate structural integrity and CQ SPARQL coverage.

**Tech Stack:** Python 3.12+, rdflib, pyshacl, pytest. Build via `uv run python ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/scripts/build.py`. Test via `uv run pytest ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/tests/ -v`.

---

## Batch 1: Requirements (CQs, SPARQL stubs, traceability)

### Task 1: Add new CQs to competency-questions.yaml

**Files:**
- Modify: `ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/docs/competency-questions.yaml`

**Step 1: Append 9 new CQ entries**

Add CQ-052 through CQ-060 at the end of the `competency_questions:` list. Follow the existing entry format exactly (see CQ-051 as template). Here are the entries:

| CQ | natural_language | type | priority | source_use_case | test_type |
|----|-----------------|------|----------|----------------|-----------|
| CQ-052 | What items were dropped during a compaction event? | Enumerative | should_have | UC-006 | SELECT non_empty |
| CQ-053 | What fraction of items were preserved during compaction? | Quantitative | should_have | UC-006 | SELECT non_empty |
| CQ-054 | When was a memory item last accessed? | Relational | should_have | UC-011 | SELECT non_empty |
| CQ-055 | Which memory items in a given tier are eligible for eviction? | Enumerative | should_have | UC-011 | SELECT non_empty |
| CQ-056 | Which session continued from a given prior session? | Relational | should_have | UC-016 | SELECT non_empty |
| CQ-057 | What is the unique identifier for a given agent, session, or conversation? | Relational | should_have | UC-001 | SELECT non_empty |
| CQ-058 | What status transitions has a task undergone? | Enumerative | should_have | UC-016 | SELECT non_empty |
| CQ-059 | What was the previous status of a session before it ended? | Relational | should_have | UC-003 | SELECT non_empty |
| CQ-060 | What event triggered a given status transition? | Relational | could_have | UC-016 | SELECT non_empty |

Each entry needs: `id`, `source_use_case`, `natural_language`, `type`, `priority`, `sample_answer` (at least one concrete example), `derivation_method`, `out_of_scope`, `sparql` (draft — will be finalized in SPARQL stubs), `expected_result`, `required_classes`, `required_properties`, `required_axioms`.

**Step 2: Verify YAML syntax**

Run: `uv run python -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/docs/competency-questions.yaml'))"`
Expected: No errors.

### Task 2: Create SPARQL test stubs

**Files:**
- Create: `ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/tests/cq-052.sparql` through `cq-060.sparql`

**Step 1: Write 9 SPARQL files**

Each file follows the same pattern as existing CQ files (see `tests/cq-041.sparql` as template). All use `PREFIX pao: <https://purl.org/pao/>` and `PREFIX prov: <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#>`.

```sparql
# cq-052.sparql — Items dropped during compaction
PREFIX pao: <https://purl.org/pao/>
PREFIX prov: <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#>
SELECT ?compaction ?item WHERE {
  ?compaction a pao:CompactionEvent .
  ?item prov:wasInvalidatedBy ?compaction .
}
```

```sparql
# cq-053.sparql — Fraction of items preserved during compaction
PREFIX pao: <https://purl.org/pao/>
PREFIX prov: <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#>
SELECT ?compaction (COUNT(?item) AS ?total) (COUNT(?dropped) AS ?droppedCount) WHERE {
  ?compaction a pao:CompactionEvent ;
              pao:compactedItem ?item .
  OPTIONAL {
    ?item prov:wasInvalidatedBy ?compaction .
    BIND(?item AS ?dropped)
  }
}
GROUP BY ?compaction
```

```sparql
# cq-054.sparql — When was a memory item last accessed?
PREFIX pao: <https://purl.org/pao/>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?item ?lastAccess WHERE {
  ?item a pao:MemoryItem ;
        pao:hasLastAccessTime ?lastAccess .
}
```

```sparql
# cq-055.sparql — Memory items eligible for eviction
PREFIX pao: <https://purl.org/pao/>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?item ?tier WHERE {
  ?item a pao:MemoryItem ;
        pao:storedIn ?tier ;
        pao:isEvictionCandidate true .
}
```

```sparql
# cq-056.sparql — Which session continued from a prior session?
PREFIX pao: <https://purl.org/pao/>
SELECT ?session ?prior WHERE {
  ?session a pao:Session ;
           pao:continuedFrom ?prior .
}
```

```sparql
# cq-057.sparql — Unique identifiers for agents, sessions, conversations
PREFIX pao: <https://purl.org/pao/>
SELECT ?entity ?id WHERE {
  { ?entity a pao:AIAgent ; pao:hasAgentId ?id . }
  UNION
  { ?entity a pao:Session ; pao:hasSessionId ?id . }
  UNION
  { ?entity a pao:Conversation ; pao:hasConversationId ?id . }
}
```

```sparql
# cq-058.sparql — Status transitions for a task
PREFIX pao: <https://purl.org/pao/>
SELECT ?task ?transition ?from ?to WHERE {
  ?transition a pao:TaskStatusTransition ;
              pao:transitionSubject ?task ;
              pao:fromStatus ?from ;
              pao:toStatus ?to .
}
```

```sparql
# cq-059.sparql — Previous status of a session before it ended
PREFIX pao: <https://purl.org/pao/>
SELECT ?session ?prevStatus WHERE {
  ?transition a pao:SessionStatusTransition ;
              pao:transitionSubject ?session ;
              pao:toStatus pao:Ended ;
              pao:fromStatus ?prevStatus .
}
```

```sparql
# cq-060.sparql — What event triggered a status transition?
PREFIX pao: <https://purl.org/pao/>
SELECT ?transition ?trigger WHERE {
  ?transition a pao:StatusTransition ;
              pao:triggeredBy ?trigger .
}
```

**Step 2: Validate SPARQL syntax**

Run: `uv run python -c "from rdflib.plugins.sparql import prepareQuery; [prepareQuery(open(f'ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/tests/cq-{n:03d}.sparql').read()) for n in range(52, 61)]; print('All valid')"`
Expected: "All valid"

## Batch 2: Conceptualization docs (glossary, conceptual model, properties, axioms)

### Task 4: Update glossary

**Files:**
- Modify: `ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/docs/glossary.csv`

**Step 1: Add new class entries**

Append these rows to `glossary.csv` (CSV format: `term,synonyms,definition,category,source_cq,bfo_category,notes`):

| term | definition | category | source_cq | bfo_category |
|------|-----------|----------|-----------|-------------|
| StatusTransition | A process in which an entity's status changes from one value to another. | class | CQ-058 | Process (BFO:0000015) |
| SessionStatusTransition | A status transition in which a session's status changes. | class | CQ-059 | Process (BFO:0000015) |
| TaskStatusTransition | A status transition in which a task's status changes. | class | CQ-058 | Process (BFO:0000015) |
| CompactionDisposition | An information artifact recording the fate of an individual item during a compaction event. | class | CQ-052 | GDC (BFO:0000031) |
| ItemFate | A status class whose individuals classify what happened to an item during compaction. | class | CQ-052 | Value Partition |

New individual entries:

| term | definition | category | source_cq |
|------|-----------|----------|-----------|
| Preserved | Item fate: content incorporated into summary. | individual | CQ-052 |
| Dropped | Item fate: excluded from summary. | individual | CQ-052 |
| Summarized | Item fate: partially represented in summary. | individual | CQ-052 |
| Archived | Item fate: moved to long-term storage. | individual | CQ-052 |

### Task 5: Update conceptual model

**Files:**
- Modify: `ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/docs/conceptual-model.yaml`

**Step 1: Add new classes to module lists**

- **pao-event module**: add `StatusTransition`, `SessionStatusTransition`, `TaskStatusTransition`
- **pao-conversation module**: add `CompactionDisposition`
- **pao-governance module (or pao-event)**: add `ItemFate`

**Step 2: Add taxonomy entries for new classes**

- `StatusTransition` parent: `Event` (BFO: Process)
- `SessionStatusTransition` parent: `StatusTransition`
- `TaskStatusTransition` parent: `StatusTransition`
- `CompactionDisposition` parent: `prov:Entity` (BFO: GDC)
- `ItemFate` parent: `Status` (Value Partition)

**Step 3: Update disjoint groups**

- Event subtypes: add `StatusTransition` to the 8-way disjoint (→ 9-way)
- StatusTransition subtypes: new disjoint pair `SessionStatusTransition`, `TaskStatusTransition`
- Status subtypes: add `ItemFate` to the 4-way disjoint (→ 5-way)

**Step 4: Update summary counts**

- total_classes: 46 → 51
- named_individuals: 17 → 21

| dispositionOf | CompactionDisposition | prov:Entity | functional | CQ-052 |
| hasItemFate | CompactionDisposition | ItemFate | functional | CQ-052 |
| continuedFrom | Session | Session | functional | CQ-056 |
| continuedBy | Session | Session | inverse of continuedFrom | CQ-056 |
| fromStatus | StatusTransition | Status | functional | CQ-058 |
| toStatus | StatusTransition | Status | functional | CQ-058 |
| transitionSubject | StatusTransition | owl:Thing | functional | CQ-058 |
| triggeredBy | StatusTransition | Event | — | CQ-060 |
| previousTransition | StatusTransition | StatusTransition | functional | CQ-058 |
| nextTransition | StatusTransition | StatusTransition | functional, inverse of previousTransition | CQ-058 |

| isEvictionCandidate | MemoryItem | xsd:boolean | functional | CQ-055 |

| hasSessionId | Session | xsd:string | functional | CQ-057 |
| hasConversationId | Conversation | xsd:string | functional | CQ-057 |

Append to the end of `imports/prov-declarations.ttl`:

```turtle
# Additional PROV-O terms for v0.3.0
prov:used a owl:ObjectProperty ; rdfs:label "used"@en ;
    rdfs:domain prov:Activity ; rdfs:range prov:Entity .
prov:wasInvalidatedBy a owl:ObjectProperty ; rdfs:label "was invalidated by"@en ;
    rdfs:domain prov:Entity ; rdfs:range prov:Activity .
prov:Derivation a owl:Class ; rdfs:label "Derivation"@en .
prov:qualifiedDerivation a owl:ObjectProperty ; rdfs:label "qualified derivation"@en ;
    rdfs:domain prov:Entity ; rdfs:range prov:Derivation .
prov:entity a owl:ObjectProperty ; rdfs:label "prov entity"@en ;
    rdfs:domain prov:Derivation ; rdfs:range prov:Entity .
prov:hadActivity a owl:ObjectProperty ; rdfs:label "had activity"@en ;
    rdfs:domain prov:Derivation ; rdfs:range prov:Activity .
```

### Task 11: Add property hierarchy, inverse pairs, and HasKey axioms

**Files:**
- Modify: `ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/scripts/build.py`

**Step 1: Add subPropertyOf in `_add_property_hierarchy()` (~line 849)**

```python
# compactedItem is subPropertyOf prov:used
g.add((PAO.compactedItem, RDFS.subPropertyOf, PROV.used))
```

**Step 2: Add inverse pairs in `_add_inverse_pairs()` (~line 864)**

Add to the `pairs` list:

```python
(PAO.continuedFrom, PAO.continuedBy),
(PAO.previousTransition, PAO.nextTransition),
```

**Step 3: Add HasKey axioms**

Add a new function `_add_has_key_axioms(g)` after `_add_disjointness_axioms`:

```python
def _add_has_key_axioms(g: Graph) -> None:
    """Add owl:hasKey axioms for identity contracts (DN-05)."""
    for cls, key_prop in [
        (PAO.AIAgent, PAO.hasAgentId),
        (PAO.Session, PAO.hasSessionId),
        (PAO.Conversation, PAO.hasConversationId),
    ]:
        key_list = BNode()
        Collection(g, key_list, [key_prop])
        g.add((cls, OWL.hasKey, key_list))
```

Call it from `build_tbox()` after `_add_disjointness_axioms(g)`:

```python
# --- HasKey identity contracts ---
_add_has_key_axioms(g)
```

### Task 12: Add existential restrictions and disjointness

**Files:**
- Modify: `ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/scripts/build.py`

**Step 1: Add existential restrictions in `_add_existential_restrictions()` (~line 884)**

Append to `restrictions` list:

```python
# CQ-052: CompactionEvent uses items
(PAO.CompactionEvent, PAO.compactedItem, PROV.Entity),
# CQ-058: StatusTransition has from/to/subject
(PAO.StatusTransition, PAO.fromStatus, PAO.Status),
(PAO.StatusTransition, PAO.toStatus, PAO.Status),
(PAO.StatusTransition, PAO.transitionSubject, OWL.Thing),
# CQ-052: CompactionDisposition has item and fate
(PAO.CompactionDisposition, PAO.dispositionOf, PROV.Entity),
(PAO.CompactionDisposition, PAO.hasItemFate, PAO.ItemFate),
```

Note: For `transitionSubject` with range `OWL.Thing`, the existing `_add_existential` helper uses `owl:someValuesFrom` which works with `owl:Thing` as filler. The test will use `filler_name=None` pattern since it's not a PAO class.

### Task 14: Add sample ABox data for new features

**Files:**
- Modify: `ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/scripts/build.py`

**Step 1: Add compaction trace data**

Create new function `_add_sample_compaction_trace(g)`:

```python
def _add_sample_compaction_trace(g: Graph) -> None:
    """Add compaction trace data linking items to preservation/drop fates."""
    compaction = PAO.compaction_001  # existing
    # Mark items as compacted inputs
    g.add((compaction, PAO.compactedItem, PAO.turn_001))
    g.add((compaction, PAO.compactedItem, PAO.turn_002))
    g.add((compaction, PAO.compactedItem, PAO.claim_preference_01))
    # Dropped items: invalidated by compaction
    g.add((PAO.turn_001, PROV.wasInvalidatedBy, compaction))
    g.add((PAO.turn_002, PROV.wasInvalidatedBy, compaction))
    # Preserved item: derived into summary
    g.add((PAO.summary_001, PROV.wasDerivedFrom, PAO.claim_preference_01))
    # Dispositions with metadata
    disp1 = PAO.disp_turn_001
    g.add((disp1, RDF.type, PAO.CompactionDisposition))
    g.add((disp1, PAO.dispositionOf, PAO.turn_001))
    g.add((disp1, PAO.hasItemFate, PAO.Dropped))
    g.add((disp1, PAO.fateReason, Literal("Low relevance to active goals")))
    g.add((compaction, PAO.hasCompactionDisposition, disp1))
    disp2 = PAO.disp_claim_pref
    g.add((disp2, RDF.type, PAO.CompactionDisposition))
    g.add((disp2, PAO.dispositionOf, PAO.claim_preference_01))
    g.add((disp2, PAO.hasItemFate, PAO.Preserved))
    g.add((disp2, PAO.fateReason, Literal("Active user preference")))
    g.add((compaction, PAO.hasCompactionDisposition, disp2))
```

**Step 2: Add eviction eligibility data**

Create new function `_add_sample_eviction(g)`:

```python
def _add_sample_eviction(g: Graph) -> None:
    """Add last-access timestamps and eviction candidacy to memory items."""
    g.add((PAO.claim_preference_01, PAO.hasLastAccessTime,
           Literal("2026-02-19T08:00:00Z", datatype=XSD.dateTime)))
    g.add((PAO.claim_preference_01, PAO.isEvictionCandidate, Literal(False)))
    g.add((PAO.episode_001, PAO.hasLastAccessTime,
           Literal("2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", datatype=XSD.dateTime)))
    g.add((PAO.episode_001, PAO.isEvictionCandidate, Literal(True)))
```

**Step 3: Add cross-session resume data**

Create new function `_add_sample_session_continuation(g)`:

```python
def _add_sample_session_continuation(g: Graph) -> None:
    """Link sessions in a continuation chain."""
    # session_002 continues from session_001
    g.add((PAO.session_002, PAO.continuedFrom, PAO.session_001))
    g.add((PAO.session_001, PAO.continuedBy, PAO.session_002))
```

**Step 4: Add identity data**

Create new function `_add_sample_identity(g)`:

```python
def _add_sample_identity(g: Graph) -> None:
    """Add unique identifiers to key entities."""
    g.add((PAO.claude_agent, PAO.hasAgentId, Literal("agent-claude-001")))
    g.add((PAO.session_001, PAO.hasSessionId, Literal("sess-001")))
    g.add((PAO.session_002, PAO.hasSessionId, Literal("sess-002")))
    g.add((PAO.conversation_001, PAO.hasConversationId, Literal("conv-001")))
```

**Step 5: Add lifecycle transition data**

Create new function `_add_sample_transitions(g)`:

```python
def _add_sample_transitions(g: Graph) -> None:
    """Add sample status transition chains."""
    # Task transitions: Pending -> InProgress -> Completed
    tt1 = PAO.task_transition_001
    g.add((tt1, RDF.type, PAO.TaskStatusTransition))
    g.add((tt1, PAO.transitionSubject, PAO.task_001))
    g.add((tt1, PAO.fromStatus, PAO.Pending))
    g.add((tt1, PAO.toStatus, PAO.InProgress))
    g.add((tt1, PAO.hasTimestamp, Literal("2026-02-19T10:00:00Z", datatype=XSD.dateTime)))
    tt2 = PAO.task_transition_002
    g.add((tt2, RDF.type, PAO.TaskStatusTransition))
    g.add((tt2, PAO.transitionSubject, PAO.task_001))
    g.add((tt2, PAO.fromStatus, PAO.InProgress))
    g.add((tt2, PAO.toStatus, PAO.Completed))
    g.add((tt2, PAO.hasTimestamp, Literal("2026-02-19T11:30:00Z", datatype=XSD.dateTime)))
    g.add((tt1, PAO.nextTransition, tt2))
    g.add((tt2, PAO.previousTransition, tt1))
    # Session transition: Active -> Ended (triggered by compaction)
    st1 = PAO.session_transition_001
    g.add((st1, RDF.type, PAO.SessionStatusTransition))
    g.add((st1, PAO.transitionSubject, PAO.session_001))
    g.add((st1, PAO.fromStatus, PAO.Active))
    g.add((st1, PAO.toStatus, PAO.Ended))
    g.add((st1, PAO.hasTimestamp, Literal("2026-02-19T12:00:00Z", datatype=XSD.dateTime)))
    g.add((st1, PAO.triggeredBy, PAO.compaction_001))
```

**Step 6: Call all new functions from `build_abox_data()`**

Add calls after the existing `_add_sample_governance_v2(g)` call:

```python
_add_sample_compaction_trace(g)
_add_sample_eviction(g)
_add_sample_session_continuation(g)
_add_sample_identity(g)
_add_sample_transitions(g)
```

**Step 7: Commit**

```bash
git add ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/scripts/build.py
git commit -m "feat(pao): v0.3.0 ABox — compaction trace, eviction, identity, transitions"
```

## Batch 5: SHACL shapes

### Task 15: Add new SHACL shapes to build.py

**Files:**
- Modify: `ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/scripts/build.py`

**Step 1: Add shapes in `build_shacl_shapes()` (~line 1862)**

Add after the existing shapes (before the `return g` statement):

```python
# --- StatusTransitionShape ---
_add_shape(
    g,
    PAO.StatusTransitionShape,
    PAO.StatusTransition,
    [
        _property_shape(g, PAO.fromStatus, min_count=1, max_count=1, class_constraint=PAO.Status),
        _property_shape(g, PAO.toStatus, min_count=1, max_count=1, class_constraint=PAO.Status),
        _property_shape(g, PAO.transitionSubject, min_count=1, max_count=1),
    ],
)

# --- CompactionDispositionShape ---
_add_shape(
    g,
    PAO.CompactionDispositionShape,
    PAO.CompactionDisposition,
    [
        _property_shape(g, PAO.dispositionOf, min_count=1, max_count=1),
        _property_shape(g, PAO.hasItemFate, min_count=1, max_count=1, class_constraint=PAO.ItemFate),
    ],
)
```

**Step 2: Update existing MemoryItemShape** (if it doesn't already have hasLastAccessTime)

Add optional `hasLastAccessTime` property shape to the existing MemoryItemShape:

```python
_property_shape(g, PAO.hasLastAccessTime, max_count=1, datatype=XSD.dateTime),
```

**Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/scripts/build.py
git commit -m "feat(pao): v0.3.0 SHACL — StatusTransition and CompactionDisposition shapes"
```

Add:


**Step 5: Update SUBPROPERTY_CHECKS**

Actually, the existing test checks `PAO[sub]` subPropertyOf `PAO[super_]`. Since `compactedItem` is `subPropertyOf prov:used` (not a PAO property), add a standalone test instead:

```python
def test_compacted_item_sub_property_of_prov_used(tbox: Graph) -> None:
    """compactedItem is subPropertyOf prov:used."""
    assert (PAO.compactedItem, RDFS.subPropertyOf, PROV.used) in tbox
```

**Step 6: Update DOMAIN_RANGE_CHECKS**

Add:

```python
# v0.3.0 additions
("compactedItem", PAO.CompactionEvent, None),  # prov:Entity range
("hasCompactionDisposition", PAO.CompactionEvent, PAO.CompactionDisposition),
("dispositionOf", PAO.CompactionDisposition, None),  # prov:Entity range
("hasItemFate", PAO.CompactionDisposition, PAO.ItemFate),
("continuedFrom", PAO.Session, PAO.Session),
("continuedBy", PAO.Session, PAO.Session),
("fromStatus", PAO.StatusTransition, PAO.Status),
("toStatus", PAO.StatusTransition, PAO.Status),
("transitionSubject", PAO.StatusTransition, None),  # owl:Thing range
("triggeredBy", PAO.StatusTransition, PAO.Event),
("previousTransition", PAO.StatusTransition, PAO.StatusTransition),
("nextTransition", PAO.StatusTransition, PAO.StatusTransition),
("fateReason", PAO.CompactionDisposition, XSD.string),
("hasLastAccessTime", PAO.MemoryItem, XSD.dateTime),
("isEvictionCandidate", PAO.MemoryItem, XSD.boolean),
("hasAgentId", PAO.AIAgent, XSD.string),
("hasSessionId", PAO.Session, XSD.string),
("hasConversationId", PAO.Conversation, XSD.string),
```

Note: Properties with `prov:Entity` or `owl:Thing` range use `None` as range (the test checks for absence of domain in that case — actually, that test pattern checks `None` for domain, not range). Need to handle this differently. For these, use the actual URIRef:

```python
("compactedItem", PAO.CompactionEvent, PROV.Entity),
("dispositionOf", PAO.CompactionDisposition, PROV.Entity),
("transitionSubject", PAO.StatusTransition, OWL.Thing),
```

The test already handles external URIRefs in the range position. Ensure `PROV` namespace is available (it already is at the top of test file).

**Step 4: Add ItemFate individual tests**

```python
def test_item_fate_individuals(ref: Graph) -> None:
    """Preserved, Dropped, Summarized, Archived are ItemFate individuals."""
    for name in ["Preserved", "Dropped", "Summarized", "Archived"]:
        assert (PAO[name], RDF.type, PAO.ItemFate) in ref


def test_item_fate_enumeration(ref: Graph) -> None:
    """ItemFate owl:oneOf (Preserved, Dropped, Summarized, Archived)."""
    members = _get_one_of_members(ref, PAO.ItemFate)
    assert members == {PAO.Preserved, PAO.Dropped, PAO.Summarized, PAO.Archived}


def test_all_different_item_fate(ref: Graph) -> None:
    """AllDifferent for ItemFate individuals."""
    assert _find_all_different_containing(
        ref, {PAO.Preserved, PAO.Dropped, PAO.Summarized, PAO.Archived}
    )
```

**Step 5: Add HasKey tests**

```python
def test_has_key_ai_agent(tbox: Graph) -> None:
    """AIAgent has owl:hasKey (hasAgentId)."""
    key_list = tbox.value(PAO.AIAgent, OWL.hasKey)
    assert key_list is not None, "AIAgent missing owl:hasKey"
    keys = list(Collection(tbox, key_list))
    assert PAO.hasAgentId in keys


def test_has_key_session(tbox: Graph) -> None:
    """Session has owl:hasKey (hasSessionId)."""
    key_list = tbox.value(PAO.Session, OWL.hasKey)
    assert key_list is not None, "Session missing owl:hasKey"
    keys = list(Collection(tbox, key_list))
    assert PAO.hasSessionId in keys


def test_has_key_conversation(tbox: Graph) -> None:
    """Conversation has owl:hasKey (hasConversationId)."""
    key_list = tbox.value(PAO.Conversation, OWL.hasKey)
    assert key_list is not None, "Conversation missing owl:hasKey"
    keys = list(Collection(tbox, key_list))
    assert PAO.hasConversationId in keys
```

## Batch 7: Build, verify, fix

### Task 20: Build and run full test suite

**Step 1: Rebuild all artifacts**

Run: `uv run python ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/scripts/build.py`

Expected output should show increased triple counts.

**Step 2: Validate Turtle**

Run: `uv run python scripts/validate_turtle.py ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/*.ttl ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/shapes/*.ttl`

Expected: All valid.

**Step 3: Lint**

Run: `uv run ruff check .`

Expected: No errors.

**Step 4: Run tests**

Run: `uv run pytest ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/tests/ -v`

Expected: All tests pass (480 + new tests). Fix any failures before proceeding.

**Step 5: Commit generated artifacts**

```bash
git add ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/personal_agent_ontology.ttl \
  ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/pao-reference-individuals.ttl \
  ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/pao-data.ttl \
  ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/shapes/pao-shapes.ttl
git commit -m "chore(pao): rebuild v0.3.0 artifacts"
```

## Verification Commands

After all tasks complete:

```bash
# Rebuild
uv run python ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/scripts/build.py

# Validate TTL
uv run python scripts/validate_turtle.py ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/*.ttl ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/shapes/*.ttl

# Lint
uv run ruff check .

# Full test suite
uv run pytest ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/tests/ -v

# ROBOT validation (if available)
robot reason --reasoner ELK --input ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/personal_agent_ontology.ttl
robot report --input ontologies/personal_agent_ontology/personal_agent_ontology.ttl --fail-on ERROR
```
